response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's crucial to be aware of the potential risks and take steps to protect yourself. Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of unverified sources: Not everything you see on social media is true. Be skeptical of information from unknown or unverified sources. 2) Verify information before sharing: Before sharing anything, make sure it's accurate by checking the source or fact-checking websites like Snopes or FactCheck.org. 3) Use fact-checking browser extensions: There are browser extensions available that can help you identify false information on social media. 4) Follow reputable news sources: Stay informed with reliable news sources to get a better understanding of current events and avoid misinformation. 5) Use privacy settings: Social media platforms have privacy settings that allow you to control who can see your posts, photos, and personal information. Use these settings to protect your privacy and security. 6) Be cautious of online relationships: Don't give out personal information to people you don't know in real life. Be cautious when meeting new people online, especially if they ask for money or try to isolate you from friends and family."
,
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the potential risks and take proactive steps. By being cautious of unverified sources, verifying information before sharing, using fact-checking browser extensions, following reputable news sources, using privacy settings, and being cautious of online relationships, individuals can protect themselves from the negative effects of misinformation.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's crucial to be aware of the potential risks and take steps to protect yourself. Here are some tips: 1) Be cautious of unverified sources: Not everything you see on social media is true. Be skeptical of information from unknown or unverified sources. 2) Verify information before sharing: Before sharing anything, make sure it's accurate by checking the source or fact-checking websites like Snopes or FactCheck.org. 3) Use fact-checking browser extensions: There are browser extensions available that can help you identify false information on social media. 4) Follow reputable news sources: Stay informed with reliable news sources to get a better understanding of current events and avoid misinformation. 5) Use privacy settings: Social media platforms have privacy settings that allow you to control who can see your posts, photos, and personal information. Use these settings to protect your privacy and security. 6) Be cautious of online relationships: Don't give out personal information to people you don't know in real life. Be cautious when meeting new people online, especially if they ask for money or try to isolate you from friends and family.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's essential to be aware of the potential risks and take proactive steps. By being cautious of unverified sources, verifying information before sharing, using fact-checking browser extensions, following reputable news sources, using privacy settings, and being cautious of online relationships, individuals can protect themselves from the negative effects of misinformation.
